title: ophrys × lydia nothosubsp. magnessa
intro: Ophrys × lydia nothosubsp. magnessa, also known as the Magness's bee orchid, is a hybrid orchid species derived from the cross between Ophrys lutea and Ophrys insectifera. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ophrys × lydia nothosubsp. magnessa include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which may help in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by drying and crushing its flowers, leaves, or roots, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures.
